title: U.S. Bancorp Series A Preferred: Trading At Discount With Over 9% Yield
text: U.S. Bancorp's Series A preferred shares are on pace to pay a dividend yield of 9.1% over the next twelve months. The bank's net interest income has declined for two consecutive quarters but remains above pre-pandemic levels. U.S. Bank's loan growth has been negative, but its allowance for credit losses covers its nonperforming loans by more than six times.

title: 4 Things You Need to Know If You Buy U.S. Bancorp Today
text: U.S. Bancorp stock is down 26% year to date amid weakness across banking sector stocks. The bank has had to bolster its capital reserves following a big acquisition last year.

title: Truist raising $1.75B in bond offering, making it the latest bank to raise capital

title: US Bank Debuts Avvance Installment Payment Tool
text: U.S. Bank has introduced a tool that lets businesses offer consumer financing at point-of-sale. The lender's Avvance offering, announced Tuesday (Oct. 24) is an “embedded, multi-channel point-of-sale lending solution” for businesses.

title: U.S. Bancorp Investors Just Got Huge News

title: U.S. Bancorp's stock rallies on release of Fed banking requirement
text: U.S. Bancorp's USB, +6.63% stock was up 5.9% after the bank said in a filing that the U.S. Federal Reserve is not requiring it to meet Category II banking requirements by Dec. 31, 2024 under the Dodd-Frank banking law.

title: U.S. Bancorp's 6.2% Yield: A Very Attractive Buy Ahead Of Q3
text: U.S. Bancorp shares are a buy-the-drop opportunity ahead of Q3 earnings, with the potential for surprise deposit growth and focus on Union Bank acquisition. The bank's deposit situation is improving, with inflows expected due to eased interest rate increases. The acquisition of Union Bank could result in cost savings and increased investor interest in USB.
